items.title: De Rotterdammer die ineens met Rui Costa en Mourinho werkt: 'Crazy verhaal'
items.summary: Sidny Lopes Cabral is in een bijna utopische wereld terechtgekomen. Ruim drie jaar geleden speelde de vleugelspeler nog op het vijfde niveau van Duitsland, maar tegenwoordig draagt hij het shirt van Benfica.

items.title: Madrid, UEFA reach deal, end Super League suits
items.summary: UEFA, European Club Football Association (EFC), and Real Madrid have reached a deal to end all legal disputes related to the Super League, UEFA and Real Madrid said on Wednesday.

items.title: Tottenham sack Frank after 8 months in charge
items.summary: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham head coach, the club confirmed Wednesday.

items.title: Sesko: Man United will fight for top-four finish
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko believes his rescue act at West Ham proves Manchester United have the stomach for the fight for a Champions League place.

items.title: Rashford to miss Copa semi, Barça injuries mount
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick lamented more bad news after Marcus Rashford joined Raphinha on the injury list for Thursday's Copa del Rey semifinal first leg against Atlético Madrid.

items.title: Einde Super League: ook Real Madrid bereikt akkoord met UEFA
items.summary: Met Real Madrid lijkt ook de laatste club de stekker uit de Super League getrokken te hebben. De Koninklijke meldt woensdagmiddag dat er 'voor het welzijn van het Europese voetbal' een akkoord is bereikt tussen Real Madrid, de UEFA en de European Football Clubs. Heel expliciet schrijft Real Madrid niet dat het uit de Super League stapt, maar dat is volgens Spaanse media wel het gevolg van het akkoord tussen Real Madrid en de Europese bond.

items.title: Clubs to vote on expanding Championship play-offs
items.summary: EFL clubs will vote on expanding play-offs in the Championship from four to six teams at a meeting next month.

items.title: 'You're getting sacked in the morning': A timeline of Thomas Frank's Tottenham reign
items.summary: Thomas Frank's time at Spurs has come to an end -- where did it all gone wrong for the Dane in north London?

items.title: Mega-interview: Jordi Cruijff deelt zijn plannen met Ajax
items.summary: Jordi Cruijff is officieel pas tien dagen begonnen, maar laat zich nu al behoorlijk gelden als technisch directeur van Ajax. De nieuwe voetbalbaas in Amsterdam stelt zich uitgebreid voor. ‘Als ik hier faal, dan heb ik: Cruijff die in de Johan Cruijff Arena faalt , dat is geen leuk verhaal.’

items.title: 'Detrimental to my season' - Hamilton bemoans Ferrari engineer situation
items.summary: Lewis Hamilton says Ferrari's failure to appoint a new permanent race engineer for him ahead of the 2026 Formula 1 season will be "detrimental" to his second campaign with the Italian team.

items.title: De cijfers die duiden wat trainer zijn van Jong Ajax zo moeilijk maakt
items.summary: 'Ik ben van mening dat een verfrissing bij Jong Ajax nodig is. Ik wil een ervaren trainer bij Jong Ajax, die een winnaarsmentaliteit kan overbrengen op de groep.' Was getekend, Jordi Cruijff. De technisch directeur van Ajax ontsloeg dinsdag Jong Ajax-trainer Willem Weijs . Zijn taak was dit seizoen niet makkelijk, zo blijkt uit de data.

items.title: Leicester tien jaar na stunt in drijfzand: 'Ik wil mijn excuses aanbieden'
items.summary: Leicester City schreef tien jaar geleden geschiedenis door tegen alle verwachtingen in de Premier League te winnen, maar tien jaar later gaat het volledig mis bij The Foxes . Interim-manager Andy King bood dinsdagavond zijn excuses aan bij de fans na een nieuw dieptepunt in een vooralsnog dramatisch seizoen.

items.title: Shot of the year? | Fritz dazzles in Dallas with behind-the-back hot shot!
items.summary: Taylor Fritz pulls off a stunning behind-the-back winner in Dallas. An early contender for shot of the year.

items.title: De Zerbi, Glasner or back to Poch? Where will Spurs turn after Frank sacking?
items.summary: Tottenham are looking for a sixth permanent manager in seven years after sacking Thomas Frank - who will they turn to next?

items.title: Tottenham’s soaring dreams for Thomas Frank quickly hit the buffers of grim reality | David Hytner
items.summary: Supposedly a pragmatic upgrade on Postecoglou, the Dane’s relationship with fans unravelled after early cup exits and league defeats in which they showed negligible attacking threat When Tottenham have made managerial changes in the 21st century, they have seemingly been guided by a specific principle. The new man must represent a fresh start and so it would surely help if he were radically different to his predecessor; often the complete counterpoint. It began when Glenn Hoddle came in for George Graham in 2001 and over the ensuing years the club have bounced, for example, from Juande Ramos to Harry Redknapp to André Villas-Boas. From Mauricio Pochettino to José Mourinho. From Antonio Conte to Ange Postecoglou. Continue reading...

items.title: Connell backing Marine Nationale to bridge Majborough gap
items.summary: Barry Connell remains confident Marine Nationale will put up a staunch defence his crown in the BetMGM Queen Mother Champion Chase at Cheltenham next month.

items.title: What went wrong for Frank?
items.summary: Thomas Frank's Tottenham spell is over after just eight months.

items.title: Why Frank at Tottenham was a gamble that ended in bitter failure
items.summary: Thomas Frank's sacking came as no surprise on Wednesday. His appointment by Tottenham was a gamble that ended in a storm of toxicity, writes Phil McNulty.

items.title: How much pressure is Slot really under at Liverpool?
items.summary: Liverpool have won just once in the Premier League in 2026 - so will they stick with Arne Slot, who took them to the title last season?

items.title: Roberto De Zerbi leaves Marseille after PSG hammering and Champions League exit
items.summary: Marseille beaten 5-0 by PSG and 3-0 by Club Brugge De Zerbi joined as head coach in 2024 after Brighton exit Roberto De Zerbi has left Marseille in the wake of a 5-0 thrashing at the hands of Paris Saint-Germain. The nine-time French champions said on Wednesday that they had ended “their collaboration by mutual agreement.” The heavy loss Sunday at the Parc des Princes restored PSG’s two-point lead over Lens after 21 rounds, with Marseille fourth. De Zerbi’s team also endured an embarrassing 3-0 loss at Club Brugge two weeks ago that resulted in Marseille exiting the Champions League. Continue reading...
